content: pyvirtualcam Camera() constructor: Camera(width, height, fps, fmt=PixelFormat.RGB, device=None). The send() method accepts 1D C-contiguous uint8 numpy array matching one of: BGR, GRAY, I420, NV12, RGB, RGBA, UYVY, YUYV. Current CameraManager.enviar_para_virtual() already converts BGR->RGB correctly before calling cam.send(). The cam.device property returns the device name as it appears in apps. On Windows, device name is 'OBS Virtual Camera' for the obs backend. sleep_until_next_frame() must be called after send() to maintain target FPS. pyvirtualcam is already correctly implemented in core/camera.py for OUTPUT — no changes needed for existing virtual cam output use.
